Ind AS vs AS (Old GAAP) — Key Differences
20 comparison areas · click any card to expand
1. Framework & Applicability
2 tags
Ind AS

Companies (Indian Accounting Standards) Rules, 2015 — applicable to listed entities, companies with net worth ≥ ₹250 cr, and certain NBFCs/Banks/Insurers. 40+ converged standards.

AS (Old GAAP)

Accounting Standards (AS 1 — AS 32) notified under Companies (Accounting Standards) Rules, 2006/2021 — applicable to all other entities. Issued by ICAI.

Financial Impact: Determines: which standards to apply, presentation format, fair value usage, and disclosure depth.
MCAInd AS Rules
2. Schedule III Format
2 tags
Ind AS

Division II of Schedule III — adds OCI section in P&L, separate Statement of Changes in Equity, and reclassifies many items. Single-step P&L format.

AS (Old GAAP)

Division I of Schedule III — older format derived from Schedule VI. No OCI or Changes in Equity statement. Multi-step P&L.

Financial Impact: Reporting: Ind AS adds two extra statements (Changes in Equity + OCI block) and substantially expanded notes.
Schedule IIIPresentation
3. Revenue Recognition
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 115 — Revenue from Contracts with Customers. Five-step model: (1) identify contract, (2) identify performance obligations, (3) determine transaction price, (4) allocate price, (5) recognise revenue when/as PO is satisfied. Variable consideration, significant financing, customer options all addressed.

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 9 — Revenue Recognition. Risks-and-rewards model for goods; reasonable certainty of ultimate collection for services. Simpler but less granular.

Financial Impact: Timing: Ind AS 115 may shift revenue earlier or later depending on PO satisfaction; variable consideration recognised earlier; bundled contracts require allocation.
Ind AS 115AS 9
4. Financial Instruments & ECL
3 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 109. Classification into Amortised Cost / FVTOCI / FVTPL. Mandatory Expected Credit Loss (ECL) model for impairment — 12-month or lifetime ECL; simplified approach for trade receivables.

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 13 — Investments: cost / market whichever lower (current) or cost less impairment (long-term). Incurred loss model for impairment — provide only when actual default has occurred.

Financial Impact: Provisions earlier: ECL typically results in higher provisions, especially for trade receivables. Equity investments at FVTOCI gains/losses go to OCI.
Ind AS 109AS 13AS 30
5. Leases
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 116. Single on-balance-sheet model for lessees — virtually all leases capitalised as Right-of-Use Assets with corresponding Lease Liability. Short-term and low-value lease exemptions.

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 19. Distinction between Finance Leases (on balance sheet) and Operating Leases (off balance sheet, expensed). Rent shown as expense.

Financial Impact: Major BS impact: Ind AS adopters see increased assets, liabilities, EBITDA, depreciation & finance cost from former operating leases.
Ind AS 116AS 19
6. Employee Benefits
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 19. Defined benefit obligations: service cost + net interest in P&L; remeasurements (actuarial gains/losses) in OCI, not recycled.

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 15. Defined benefit obligations: actuarial gains/losses in P&L immediately. No OCI concept.

Financial Impact: Volatility: AS shows actuarial volatility in PAT; Ind AS isolates it in OCI — cleaner reported earnings but TCI same.
Ind AS 19AS 15
7. PPE — Component Accounting
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 16. Mandatory component accounting — significant parts of an asset depreciated separately based on their own useful lives.

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 10 (Rev. 2016) also requires component accounting for newer adoptions, but historically simpler. Schedule II useful lives apply.

Financial Impact: Depreciation: Component approach often results in differential depreciation patterns; major spare parts capitalised.
Ind AS 16AS 10
8. Business Combinations
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 103 — Acquisition Method. All identifiable assets/liabilities at fair value. Goodwill is not amortised, subject to annual impairment test. Bargain purchase to OCI/Capital Reserve.

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 14 — Amalgamations. Pooling of interests allowed (for amalgamation in nature of merger). Goodwill amortised over 5 years normally.

Financial Impact: Post-acquisition profits may differ: Ind AS no goodwill amortisation but impairment shock; AS shows steady amortisation.
Ind AS 103AS 14
9. Consolidation
4 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 110 — Control model (power + variable returns + ability to affect returns). Joint Ventures equity-method only (Ind AS 111). NCI at fair value or proportionate share.

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 21 — Ownership threshold (>50% voting rights). JVs use proportionate consolidation (AS 27). NCI at proportionate share of net assets.

Financial Impact: Consolidation scope may change for SPVs / structured entities — Ind AS captures more.
Ind AS 110Ind AS 111AS 21AS 27
10. Income Taxes
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 12 — Balance Sheet approach. Deferred tax based on temporary differences (BS carrying value vs tax base). Wider scope including business combinations, fair value adjustments.

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 22 — Income Statement approach. Deferred tax based on timing differences in P&L items only.

Financial Impact: DTL/DTA may differ materially — Ind AS captures more items (revaluation, undistributed profits of subsidiaries etc.).
Ind AS 12AS 22
11. Provisions, Contingent Liabilities & Assets
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 37. Discounting mandatory if time value of money is material. Onerous contracts recognised.

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 29. Discounting not permitted. Onerous contract concept absent.

Financial Impact: Liability measurement: Long-term provisions (decommissioning, warranty) measured at PV under Ind AS; higher finance cost over time.
Ind AS 37AS 29
12. Fair Value Measurement
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 113. Defines fair value as exit price in an orderly transaction. 3-level hierarchy (Level 1: quoted, Level 2: observable inputs, Level 3: unobservable).

AS (Old GAAP)

No equivalent standard. Fair value referenced piecemeal in individual standards (AS 13, AS 28, etc.).

Financial Impact: Disclosure: Ind AS adopters give extensive fair value hierarchy tables; AS users have minimal FV disclosures.
Ind AS 113—
13. Government Grants
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 20. Grants cannot be credited to capital reserve. Income approach (deferred income) or asset approach (deduct from asset cost).

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 12. Grants related to non-depreciable assets credited to Capital Reserve permitted.

Financial Impact: Reserves vs P&L: Ind AS recognises grants over useful life via P&L; AS may park them in equity permanently.
Ind AS 20AS 12
14. Borrowing Costs
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 23. Capitalise borrowing costs attributable to qualifying assets. Largely converged with AS 16.

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 16. Same principle — capitalise borrowing costs directly attributable.

Financial Impact: Minor: Most differences relate to the population of qualifying assets and treatment of foreign exchange differences.
Ind AS 23AS 16
15. Share-Based Payments
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 102. All share-based payments at grant-date fair value. Modifications, cash-settled, and cash alternatives all addressed.

AS (Old GAAP)

No corresponding AS — ICAI Guidance Note on ESOPs / Intrinsic value method commonly used. Fair value not mandatory.

Financial Impact: Higher ESOP expense: Ind AS typically results in materially higher employee benefit charge than intrinsic-value method.
Ind AS 102ICAI GN
16. OCI & Total Comprehensive Income
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 1. OCI is mandatory. Total Comprehensive Income = PAT + OCI. Items reclassifiable vs not reclassifiable.

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 1. No OCI concept. All gains/losses through P&L or directly in equity (e.g. revaluation reserve).

Financial Impact: Earnings volatility: Ind AS isolates one-off remeasurements in OCI for cleaner PAT; TCI captures the full picture.
Ind AS 1—
17. Cash Flow Statement
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 7. Interest paid/received and dividend paid/received classification is policy choice (Operating/Investing/Financing).

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 3. Interest paid → Financing; Interest/Dividend received → Investing; Dividend paid → Financing (for non-financial entities).

Financial Impact: Operating CF can differ — Ind AS allows greater classification flexibility but requires consistency.
Ind AS 7AS 3
18. Segment Reporting
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 108. Management approach — segments as reviewed internally by CODM (Chief Operating Decision Maker).

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 17. Risks-and-rewards approach — pre-defined business/geographical segments.

Financial Impact: Segment count & definitions may change; Ind AS aligns external reporting with internal management view.
Ind AS 108AS 17
19. Related Party Disclosures
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 24. Broader definition — covers KMP and their close family members, post-employment benefit plans, joint operations. Government-related entity exemption.

AS (Old GAAP)

AS 18. Narrower scope — primarily KMP, holding/subsidiary/associate.

Financial Impact: More disclosures under Ind AS — close family transactions, executive compensation details.
Ind AS 24AS 18
20. First-Time Adoption
2 tags
Ind AS

Ind AS 101. Comprehensive first-time adoption standard: mandatory exceptions (estimates, hedge accounting, NCI etc.) and optional exemptions (business combinations, fair value as deemed cost, etc.).

AS (Old GAAP)

No equivalent. Transition between AS versions handled via specific transitional provisions in each standard.

Financial Impact: First Ind AS FS needs reconciliation of equity and TCI from previous GAAP — extensive disclosure.
Ind AS 101—
